name: health-insurance-enrollment
description: Marketplace enrollment, special enrollment periods, Medicaid expansion, CHIP, and COBRA alternatives. For the acute 'I just lost my coverage' crisis.
source: Liberation Labs mutual aid knowledge base

triples.subject: job loss
triples.object: special enrollment period of 60 days to enroll in marketplace health insurance — clock starts from date coverage ends, not last day of work

triples.subject: COBRA continuation coverage
triples.object: marketplace plan with income-based subsidies — always compare costs before defaulting to COBRA

triples.subject: income below 138% federal poverty level
triples.object: Medicaid in expansion states (41 states + DC as of 2026) — application can be done same-day at local DSS

triples.subject: children under 19
triples.object: CHIP (Children's Health Insurance Program) at higher income thresholds than adult Medicaid — covers dental and vision

triples.subject: marketplace premium subsidy
triples.object: households earning 100-400% FPL (expanded through 2025 legislation) — calculated on a sliding scale

triples.subject: community health center
triples.object: sliding-scale primary care regardless of insurance status — federally funded, cannot turn patients away for inability to pay

triples.subject: special enrollment period
triples.object: marriage, divorce, birth/adoption, moving to new coverage area, aging off parent's plan, losing Medicaid eligibility

triples.subject: marketplace application
triples.object: healthcare.gov (federal), state marketplace websites, by phone (1-800-318-2596), or with in-person navigator assistance

triples.subject: navigator or certified application counselor
triples.object: free help with marketplace enrollment — trained, certified, and prohibited from charging fees

triples.subject: cost-sharing reduction (CSR)
triples.object: deductibles, copays, and out-of-pocket maximums for enrollees earning 100-250% FPL — only available on Silver tier plans

triples.subject: Medicaid application
triples.object: retroactive coverage for up to 3 months before application date — covers medical bills incurred before enrollment

triples.subject: presumptive eligibility
triples.object: temporary Medicaid coverage while full application is processed — available at hospitals and qualified providers

triples.subject: COBRA election period
triples.object: 60 days from notification — can elect retroactively to cover gap, but must pay all premiums back to coverage loss date

triples.subject: losing marketplace subsidy
triples.object: income increases mid-year — report income changes promptly to avoid owing subsidy repayment at tax time

triples.subject: gap in coverage
triples.object: short-term health plans (limited coverage, check exclusions), health sharing ministries, or Medicaid if eligible

triples.subject: dental coverage for adults
triples.object: some marketplace plans, standalone dental plans, dental schools (reduced-cost care), and Medicaid in some states

triples.subject: mental health parity law
triples.object: marketplace and employer plans to cover mental health and substance use treatment at the same level as medical/surgical care

triples.subject: prescription drug coverage
triples.object: check the plan formulary before enrolling — the cheapest premium plan may not cover your medications

triples.subject: young adults
triples.object: parent's health insurance until age 26 regardless of student status, employment, marital status, or financial dependence

triples.subject: immigration status
triples.object: lawfully present immigrants can buy marketplace plans; undocumented immigrants cannot enroll but emergency Medicaid covers emergencies regardless of status
